framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Lean / Toyota Production System practice and teaching
paths.what_it_is: Deep, hands-on mastery of Toyota-style lean manufacturing (value stream mapping, kaizen, scientific-thinking coaching) codified into books, courses, or consulting that others use to run their own plants.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows the engineer can turn tacit shop-floor craft into a repeatable system that other organizations can actually pick up and run.

paths.what_it_is: Building and publishing a rigorous, statistically grounded methodology for eliminating defects and variation, then training others to apply it across industries.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves the engineer can make quality improvement measurable and teachable rather than a matter of individual intuition.

paths.path: Design of experiments (DOE) rigor for process improvement
paths.what_it_is: Using formal statistical experiment design to isolate which process variables actually drive quality and yield, instead of changing one factor at a time by guesswork.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows the engineer can extract a correct causal answer from a noisy process with the fewest possible experimental runs, a skill that scales far beyond any one product line.
